title: Lesson Quiz: Credit and Debt
description: Assessment of the concept that Loans, interest, and financial responsibility.

choices.text: The ability to borrow money now and repay it later
choices.feedback: Correct. Credit allows a person to borrow now and repay later.

choices.text: Money set aside in a savings account
A type of monthly budget category
choices.feedback: Credit is not a budget category.

choices.text: A rule for comparing product prices

questions.prompt: Interest rate means the percentage charged for borrowing money.

choices.feedback: Correct. The interest rate is the percentage charged on borrowed money.
This is incorrect. That is the meaning of interest rate in this lesson.
Correct. Debt is money that must be repaid to a lender.

choices.text: Money earned from working a job
A reward for using a budget
Money earned as interest on savings
choices.feedback: That describes interest income, not debt.

choices.text: Because borrowing creates repayment responsibilities and interest costs
choices.feedback: Correct. Borrowing should be managed carefully because the loan must be repaid with added cost.

choices.text: Because all loans always have no effect on future finances
choices.feedback: Loans can affect future spending and financial stability.
Borrowing creates debt; it does not automatically increase savings.

choices.text: Because interest rates make every loan free over time
choices.feedback: Interest increases the cost of borrowing rather than making a loan free.

questions.prompt: Making payments on time is part of responsible use of credit.

choices.feedback: Correct. On-time repayment is an important part of financial responsibility.
This is incorrect. Responsible borrowing includes making payments on time.
